title: Blacklisting dumb things LLMs say about trading.
author: fibspeak
selftext:
I'm working on a series of toolkit assisted agents that can execute set rules trades on varying levels of complex TA patterns. 

I want these to run in two modes. One is a verbose blog style analysis. This can be used for personal auditing of the work and also it can run things for public facing content. 

Secondly, I want all of this to be captured in clean distinct rules that are actionable trade plans. 

I want my plans to look like this. 

https://preview.redd.it/uw4e9kc1g23h1.png?width=1549&format=png&auto=webp&s=5e0c795c051425b4a1e795a0c3a736eaaeec9e71

  
Reads well to a human and easily atomizes the important data of price levels and if then plans to be able to automatically populate python based execution tools. 

The problem is, LLMs learn the patterns of writing analysis from humans and most humans who do "professional analysis" and actually "professional fence-sitters".  So many terms are popular that have no hard underlying meaning - not one that is easily discernible and broken into useful rules.

  
"Traders should keep an eye on the news because (big news) may reverse the trend" 

\- Say what? So what do we do here? "Keep an eye on the news". Then what? Close trades if we think news is coming? Close them if news comes? Is simply "Keeping an eye" on it the task? What does this even mean?  Nothing .... it means nothing. It's an escape hatch. Then if someone says something and something else happens they can say "Ahhh, but I said about the news stuff". 

  
"Monitor price for a switch in momentum" 

And WTF is that? It sounds like its saying something but its not. "Momentum" here is one word aiming to encapsulate something that takes many words to define. Momentum is a property of trend type, structure of trend, phase of trend and then some interpretation of  a differentiator  between expected momentum in a retracement vrs adverse momentum in a reversal. You could write books on this subject, "Switch in momentum"  ain't cutting it. 



"Wait for consolidation" 

That's a popular one huh. Casually condensing another book into a word and acting like we should all know what that means.  What's the execution rules? Do we wait for "confirmation" and retest? Do we enter after confirmation? If we wait on retest how do we know when its "too much confirmation" (missed the trade). If we enter after the trigger candle how do we account for the inconsistent RR to establish an edge? It's entirely incomplete. 

  
"Pay close attention to to FOMC minutes" 

Sure. Market is gonna move 100s of points in less time than i can click a button but I'll be keeping a keen eye on those minutes to make my trade updates. GTFO!



====

These types of useless phrases appear in LLM analysis a lot because they appear in human analysis a lot, and most humans are just trying to keep their job and not look stupid. For that purpose these vague, ill-defined and hedging words are nice to hide behind. But if you're actually acting on it, this is all useless. 

And its gonna appear in the LLM content because its highly weighted in the training set and the LLM "wants" to complete loops. 

These popular tropes appear in so manty different forms of analysis and the LLM has a tendency to generate; 

What it is. 

Why it is. 

What it means. 

What it tells us. 

  
Which assumes perfect causality in markets. That things are never paradoxical and that these filler phrases people tag onto work rather than learn enough to put in real position management strategies aren't just pointless catchphrases. None of that is true in reality, but its entirely true inside of the training set of a LLM. 

When really what we need for a trade plan is; 

What it is. 

What we infer from it. 

What we plan to do in different outcomes. 

And why is irrelevant.  You do not get paid for knowing "why" something happened. You're not docked profits for not being able to submit a questionnaire about what exactly Trump said to make the price move. The market functionally does not care about these things. Those who talk about it after were never in the trade before-  so who cares? 

  
Any time you let the LLM drift into narrative writing it will write something dumb, because 99% of narratives written about trading are dumb.

comments.author: MycoHost01
comments.body:
This is what mine is saying 

TRADE 6 ─────────────────────────  
Wintermist  
Trader   Excalibur  
SHORT SOL · 10x

Entry    $86.5862  May-24 11:37 UTC  
Stop     $87.20    (+0.71%)  
Target   $84.92    (-1.92%)  
Size     Coll $989.81 · Notional $9,898.10

Thesis  
The 4-hour RSI recently exited oversold (56.15) but the price is now at 86.59, already above the 4-hour EMA50 of 86.43, suggesting a potential mean-reversion rejection. The 5-min RSI7 is neutral at 61.25, but the flow split shows 60/40 long bias which often precedes a correction. The largest short-side liquidation cluster at 86.84 (443k) sits just above current price, providing a near-term magnet for short entries, while the largest long-side clusters below (85.57, 85.49) represent potential downside targets. A whale long at 86.62 15min ago may have been filled near local highs. Entering a short with a 87.20 invalidation (above the prior day's swing and cluster) and targeting 84.92 (large long liq cluster) gives \~1:2.5 RR.

─────────────────────────────────

\---

EXCALIBUR — Cycle 27 (epoch 45)  
TRADE OPENED #50 | SHORT 10x conf 0.62  
entry $86.59 | stop $87.20 | target $84.92

replies.author: fibspeak
replies.body:
i can do that with sterile outputs telling it just to focus on TA / certain things but what im doing is assigning characters to the LLMs (permabull etc). Giving them access to my research lib, my strategies and  tools to run the run strategies and then getting them to long form thesis' branching off into different trade branches (tracking overall trade plans and contingencies rather than one signal). 

  
Assigning them characters gives them some creative licence and they start to say dumb stuff that needs blacklisted.

replies.author: MycoHost01
replies.body: am still having a hard time as to what you trying to do. apologies. like you want the outputs of the llm to be more detailed than this ""Monitor price for a switch in momentum"" iv got those types of replies when im just providing snippets of data and asking for advice as to what too look for theres zero harness on it so it just gives a generic response and not the whole thing. im indexing my data and made a custom harness so it knows exactly what kind of momentum it should monitor the harness is built for what im doing etc. i have two separations one is the trader and the other is the reporter that formats everything any of the agents i have and outputs into a readable report for a human to follow if i want recommendations if im trading too the whole set up is for the llm to trade but i also want recommendations so i set up the reporter for exactly that but they all have access to market data all the data they need to verify and say what they need to say in detail and what to look out for it knows its trading in sol it knows the btc data i have is to be used as context since it knows btc moves assets and it knows the venue we are trading at etc. i also get this from my reporter ""Pay close attention to to FOMC minutes"" but in more detailed format for me for the trader it formats it different no interpretation just data for the trader to act on

replies.author: fibspeak
replies.body:
\>having a hard time as to what you trying to do

i have a research body of work that looks at all the major booms and busts in the 140 yrs of indices history.  this is the base source of truth for odds in different phases of trend development. 

then i have a series of hardcoded strategies that are things ive learned from 20 yrs of manual trading. 

the LLMs can access the core research data, they can access all the strategies and then they can select the correct strategy as per their bias. A momentum trader will always use the trend strats, a counter trader will always use the fades etc.

that all works great. the llms create the signal posts and the signals are profitable and close mirrors of how id execute the same. 

it just occasionally adds these filler phrases (which ive wrote a blacklist for). 

its more a commentary on how shallow the "analysis" that people wrote that went into the training data than the llm's capabilities.
